claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: New strong bitcoin emphasis not anticipated by the Ethereum-centric prediction: advocating ZK/SNARKs 'back home to bitcoin', a VERIFY_SNARK opcode to make bitcoin programmable, and arguing bitcoin's security model must change.
Shipped Lasso (new lookup argument ~10x faster than halo2) and Jolt — an explicitly ISA-/chain-agnostic zkVM (EVM, RISC-V, MIPS), a concrete major SNARK launch broader than Ethereum.

Deepfakes / AI-content provenance became a major new applied focus for crypto+ZK (long July 22 thread, Dec 17), shifting AI+crypto toward provenance/anti-fakery rather than the 'autonomous 0-person protocol' framing in the prediction.
Championed reframing 'Data Availability' as 'Data Publishing'/'Data Storage' — a new terminological/conceptual stance.
Took a notably favorable, defended stance on Worldcoin / World ID biometric proof-of-personhood as a complement to social-graph approaches.
Broadened beyond Ethereum L2: openness to app-specific chains, alternative VMs (Move, Rust) and MEV internalization (Sept 19 podcast), plus exploring Aleo's Leo private-chain stack.
notes: All six sector-excitement theses and the risk-on builder posture were strongly confirmed, often via concrete action: Lasso/Jolt and Halmos (ZK/security), onchain-summer + Oct-27 'high-quality blockspace is compute-bound' (L2/blockspace), 'fully on-chain games are a favorite category' + CCP/EVE playtest promotion + playing Primodium (games), defending Farcaster vs Nostr and engaging @dwr (social), smart accounts/passkeys/MPC and proof-of-personhood (identity), deepfake provenance and 'crypto-the-computer vs casino' (AI+crypto). Risk_regime confirmed by tech-tree/long-arc framing, 'timeline full of fascinating projects', TVL-is-a-bad-metric, and the forward-looking 2024 big-ideas piece. Concern axis fared worse: only 'closed centralized platforms' was revisited (Apple/Beeper sarcasm Dec 19, 'centralization limits the speed of innovation'); opaque exchanges/custodians (only a faint self-custody throughline in the Sept 11 secrets piece), pie-chart tokens (the Aug 14 TVL-bad-metric tweet is the related vanity-metrics theme, not the same stance), and U.S. regulation were not addressed = absent. Token axis is sparse by design: ETH conviction persists (onchain summer, EVM/Solidity network-effects discussion) though notably broadened toward multi-chain/bitcoin/alt-VMs and explicitly 'may not run away and win'; MKR never came up. No claims were contradicted.
